top_five.rationale: Listed parent (HKEX:752, HK$5.3B FY23 rev, 2,300 staff) out-resources Elitez on enterprise tenders; trusted by SG government + global brands. Score: 5 x (6-2) = 20.
MBS / RWS / Shangri-La banquet teams at SGD 100-200/pax bypass agency margin entirely; the default substitute SG buyers default to without an agency in the room.
Owns Elitez's exact ICP: D&D + family day + team building for Google, DBS, Grab, Singtel, NTUC, KPMG. 1,000+ events since 2014, 4.9/5 across 200+ reviews.
Team-building + family-day specialist with proprietary game IP (Bumball); 500+ Fortune 500 + gov reviews; tournament format scales to Elitez's 50-1,500 PAX sweet spot.
Internal corp-comms / HR teams running the event direct save the 12-20% agency fee; the 'do-nothing' NBA that kills mid-market RFPs before Elitez sees them.

competitors.name: Get Out! Events (GO Events)
competitors.pricing_range_published: Family day SGD 5,000-30,000; D&D SGD 80-250/pax; corporate event SGD 25,000-70,000
competitors.primary_value_prop: End-to-end SG corporate event agency with deep D&D, team building and family day playbooks.
strengths: Marquee SG corporate logos: Google, DBS, Grab, Singtel, NTUC, Shopee, KFC, Pfizer, KPMG, Daikin, RHB (per getout.sg, 2026-04)
1,000+ corporate events since 2014; 4.9/5 average across 200+ Google reviews (per getout.sg)
Published cost guides 2025/2026 dominate organic SEO for 'corporate event Singapore' queries
weaknesses: Pricing is 'contact us' — mid-market buyers cannot self-qualify
Single-country footprint (SG only); cannot serve regional rollouts
No FMCG promoter or temp-staffing arm — must subcontract crew (Elitez ecosystem advantage)
competitors.website_design_notes: Hero leads with 'SG #1 Event Co' + 4 KPI badges (1,000+ events, 13yrs, 4.9 stars, 500+ co); 30+ blue-chip logos above fold (Google, DBS, Grab, Singtel); 'Get a Free Quote' CTA visible; budget calculator removes pricing mystery — best conversion path in the set.

competitors.name: JNR Entertainment
competitors.pricing_range_published: Tight-budget positioning; 'all-in-house' to keep below market rate
competitors.primary_value_prop: Budget-conscious D&D and family day specialist with in-house equipment and talent.
strengths: Sentosa Development Corporation's Appointed Event Planner (per jnrentertainment.com.sg) — distribution moat at Sentosa venues
In-house equipment and talent reduces vendor mark-up on small-to-mid budget D&D
Published positioning explicitly targets 'tight budget' segment Elitez also fights for
weaknesses: Brand reads 'entertainment / DJ-led' rather than full-service strategic agency
Limited public case studies for blue-chip corporate buyers Elitez targets (NIE, SIT, Hermes)
Pricing entirely hidden; cannot pre-qualify buyers

competitors.name: BON Events
competitors.pricing_range_published: D&D package — published page exists, exact figures gated
competitors.primary_value_prop: Singapore D&D specialist since 2007 with packaged dinner & dance offering.
strengths: 18-year track record specifically on D&D (since 2007 per bon-events.com)
Productised 'D&D Package Singapore' lets buyers self-serve faster than RFP
Specialist focus = repeat-business referral loop
weaknesses: Narrow vs. Elitez 6-category scope — light on family day, brand activations
Smaller team / limited concurrent capacity
No published reference logos at the tier of Hermes, BytePlus, SingHealth

competitors.name: Tembusu Events
competitors.pricing_range_published: Bespoke; AR/VR add-ons priced as separate line items
competitors.primary_value_prop: Tech-forward corporate event planner using AR/VR, mobile apps for D&D and gala.
strengths: Differentiated AR/VR + custom-app positioning (per tembusuevents.com.sg)
Dual capability: PCO conference work + corporate D&D widens deal pipeline
Established 2012; 13-year operating history
weaknesses: Tech add-ons inflate price beyond Elitez's mid-market sweet spot
Hidden pricing slows velocity
No FMCG promoter / staffing arm — must subcontract
competitors.website_design_notes: AIA/Allianz/P&G/HDB logos + ISO27001/BizSafe; but multiple SVG hero placeholders fail to render (broken image pipeline), repetitive 'event company SG' copy, three competing CTAs, no AR/VR demos despite tagline; mobile/perf undermined by broken assets.

competitors.name: Electric Dreamz
competitors.pricing_range_published: 'Low cost / quality one-stop' — figures gated to enquiry
competitors.primary_value_prop: Low-cost one-stop D&D and corporate event provider for SG SMEs.
strengths: Explicit low-cost positioning (per electricdreamz.com homepage)
One-stop bundling pitched as TCO-lower vs. multi-vendor
Direct phone contact (+65 8586 0502) — fast SME response loop
weaknesses: Brand reads SME-only; limited proof on enterprise / blue-chip buyers
'Low cost' positioning compresses margin
No regional footprint

competitors.name: Mariko Venture
competitors.pricing_range_published: Affordability-positioned; non-obligatory advisory model
competitors.primary_value_prop: Affordable one-stop event planner for SG corporate, school and community events.
strengths: 'Synonymous with affordability' framing wins price-sensitive RFPs
Non-obligatory advisory before brief signed lowers buyer activation energy
Multi-segment (schools + community) gives counter-cyclical revenue mix
weaknesses: School / community work dilutes corporate brand premium
No published heavyweight corporate logos
Hidden pricing; cannot self-qualify

competitors.name: INX Events & Productions
competitors.pricing_range_published: Bespoke; brand-experience tier pricing
competitors.primary_value_prop: Brand experience and events agency covering conceptualisation through post-event publicity.
strengths: Named Economist Impact and ADK Connect as long-term clients (per inx.com.sg testimonials)
Marketing+events hybrid (brandactivation.com.sg sister site) covers full funnel
Operating since 2012 — 13 years' track record
weaknesses: Brand-led pitch attracts marketing budget, not D&D budget
Pricing opaque to mid-market HR buyers
Limited family day proof points
competitors.website_design_notes: H1 'ABOUT INX' buried mid-page (not hero); SVG hero renders blank in source; 'Successful Activations: 0' placeholder undermines authority; named testimonials (Economist Impact, ADK Connect) saved it; neutral grey palette has no accent; phone/email buried.

competitors.name: Twist Media
competitors.pricing_range_published: 1,000+ events delivered; rate card not published
competitors.primary_value_prop: Multi-format SG event agency from gala dinners to outdoor carnivals and hybrid events.
strengths: Self-reports 1,000+ events delivered (per twistmedia.sg)
Format breadth (gala, carnival, exhibition, hybrid) maps to 5 of Elitez's 6 categories
Active LinkedIn presence — recruiting signal of growth
weaknesses: Pricing entirely hidden — friction for SME self-serve
No public ecosystem-arm story (manpower, security) to match Elitez Group's stack
Generic 'event agency' positioning vs. Elitez's vertical depth

competitors.name: Macro Events
competitors.pricing_range_published: Bespoke; quote-on-brief
competitors.primary_value_prop: Singapore D&D and corporate event planner serving MNC and SMB clients.
strengths: Self-positions as 'one of Singapore's most trusted' event planning partners
Dual MNC + SMB targeting widens funnel
Strong organic SEO presence on D&D queries
weaknesses: Generic positioning blurs differentiation
No published headline reference clients
No staffing/promoter ecosystem — pure agency model

competitors.name: Mega Events
competitors.pricing_range_published: Bespoke; sales contact 8061 3416
competitors.primary_value_prop: Singapore D&D specialist publishing annual planning guides for corporate buyers.
strengths: Publishes annual D&D planning guide (2025) — content marketing builds inbound
Direct sales line (8061 3416) for fast SME contact
Specialist depth on D&D format
weaknesses: Narrower than Elitez's 6-category footprint
Smaller team per LinkedIn
Hidden pricing

competitors.name: Rave Productions
competitors.pricing_range_published: Bespoke; 30-year heritage premium
competitors.primary_value_prop: 30-year veteran D&D and corporate event planner with advanced production tech.
strengths: 30 years' operating history (per raveproductions.com.sg) — longest tenure in this set
5-star rating across review platforms cited on site
Direct number (+65 6282 8712) for enterprise sales
weaknesses: Heritage brand may read 'safe but uninspired' to younger SG corporates
No published recent rebrand or AI/AR feature push
Pricing opaque
competitors.website_design_notes: 30-yr heritage tagline + 'OUR VALUED CLIENTS' logo strip; multi-channel contact (phone/email/WhatsApp); but data:image/gif placeholders, no testimonials/certs/dates, basic form styling, limited color hierarchy — reads dated despite functional structure.

competitors.name: Pixo Events Asia
competitors.pricing_range_published: Project-based; quote-on-brief
competitors.primary_value_prop: Healthcare / medical-leaning corporate event organiser with in-house production and livestream.
strengths: Healthcare/medical specialism (per pixo.sg) directly competes for NUH, SingHealth, TTSH, NKF logos Elitez also touts
In-house production + livestream + design (no subcontracting)
20-year operating history
weaknesses: Conference / hybrid focus understates D&D and family day capability
No proven brand activation depth
Hidden pricing
competitors.website_design_notes: H1 'SG's Leading Event Mgmt & PCO' + '20+ years/250+ clients/5-star' badges; but only WhatsApp CTA in hero (no consult CTA), 3 emails/3 phones cause decision paralysis, email-protection cdn-cgi shows obfuscation, no medical case studies despite healthcare niche.

competitors.name: Funco Events
competitors.pricing_range_published: Tier-by-event; published cost guides for orientation
competitors.primary_value_prop: Tech-and-sustainability event company that has serviced 670+ SG companies.
strengths: 670+ SG company client base (per event.funco.com.sg) — broad penetration
Sustainability angle increasingly demanded by ESG-driven SG corporates
Publishes pricing benchmarks → captures SEO long-tail
weaknesses: Sustainability framing not yet a primary purchase criterion for D&D buyers
Heritage carnival-rental positioning lingers in brand
No regional footprint

competitors.name: PartyMojo
competitors.pricing_range_published: Per-station and per-package SGD price lists published
competitors.primary_value_prop: 300+ in-house carnival, food and game services for family day and team building events.
strengths: 300+ in-house event services (per partymojo.com.sg) — true single-vendor consolidation for family day
Published SGD price lists for game booths, food stations — fastest self-quote in the set
3-day proposal turnaround for corporate family day
weaknesses: Family day / carnival vertical only — no D&D, gala, or brand activation depth
Brand reads 'kid-party' which can deter senior corporate buyers
No regional / multi-country offering

competitors.name: Cohesion Singapore
competitors.pricing_range_published: Per-pax stations; from SGD 8/cup Bumball minimum 20
competitors.primary_value_prop: Decade-old SG team building and family day operator with proprietary game formats.
strengths: 500+ public Facebook/Google reviews from Fortune 500 + gov agencies (per cohesion.sg)
Proprietary game IP (Bumball) gives differentiation small competitors can't copy
Tournament format scales 50-1,500 PAX — Elitez's exact sweet spot
weaknesses: Team building / family day specialist — does not credibly pitch D&D or awards ceremony
No brand activation arm
Limited regional reach
competitors.website_design_notes: Hero 'BONDING THROUGH PLAY' + action photography; 900+ co/100k people/4.9 stars/500+ reviews + Fortune 500 logos (Google, Apple, Amazon, Meta, HSBC, PayPal); 'Plan My Event' CTA appears 8x, WhatsApp pre-filled; teal/charcoal palette, WebP images, hamburger nav — top-tier set.

competitors.name: Visionnaire
competitors.pricing_range_published: Premium tier; ISO 20121 + EcoVadis Gold premium
competitors.primary_value_prop: Luxury and automotive launch specialist, ISO 20121 + EcoVadis Gold credentialed.
strengths: ISO 20121 sustainability + EcoVadis Gold (per visionnaire.com.sg) — wins ESG-screened RFPs
Luxury / automotive niche commands premium margins Elitez does not currently access
Operating 17+ years (incorporated 2007 per ACRA-equivalent records)
weaknesses: Premium niche shrinks PAX volume — not aligned to Elitez 1,500-PAX scale
Boutique team capacity
Limited family day / team building proof

competitors.name: Pico Singapore
competitors.hq: Hong Kong (Pico Far East Holdings; SG office Kallang)
competitors.pricing_range_published: Enterprise tier; HK$5.3B FY23 group revenue
competitors.primary_value_prop: Listed full-service event, exhibition and brand activation network with end-to-end SG delivery.
strengths: Listed parent (HKEX:752); FY23 revenue HK$5,327.9M (+17.3% YoY); 2,300 staff (per pico.com 2023 annual results)
SG office at 20 Kallang Avenue + dedicated Pico Creative Centre
Trusted by global leading brands, corporates and SG government (per pico.com)
weaknesses: Enterprise pricing structurally above Elitez's mid-market band
Long approval chain — slower SME response than local boutique
Exhibition/booth-build legacy can over-engineer simple D&D briefs
competitors.website_design_notes: Bot-walled by Akamai; auditing from cached patterns. Enterprise template — generic global hero, sparse SG-specific content, trust via implied scale (HK$5.3B parent, HKEX:752) but no SG client logos above fold; CTA is a generic enquiry; modern responsive but not state-of-art.

competitors.name: Cityneon (NEON)
competitors.pricing_range_published: Enterprise; private equity backed (West Knighton / CVC era)
competitors.primary_value_prop: Singapore-rooted exhibitions and immersive experiences network now operating as NEON.
strengths: Singapore HQ at 84 Genting Lane (per sgmeetings.com listing) — local + global stack
Themed-entertainment portfolio (Marvel/Avatar IPs) gives experiential pedigree
Globally-scaled supply chain
weaknesses: Pivoted heavily to themed entertainment / IP licensing — less focus on annual corporate D&D
Heavy fixed-cost base reflected in pricing
Brand confusion post-rename Cityneon -> NEON

competitors.name: MP International
competitors.pricing_range_published: PCO retainer + per-event; enterprise tier
competitors.primary_value_prop: 30+ year SG-rooted PCO and global event agency for government, association and medical events.
strengths: Best PCO in Asia at M&C Asia Stella Awards 2025 (per mpinetwork.com)
250+ national, regional and international events delivered
Government + medical congress credentials directly compete for SG public-sector RFPs
weaknesses: Conference / PCO orientation — less native to D&D and family day
Enterprise approval cycles slow vs. Elitez SME response
Premium positioning
competitors.website_design_notes: Best PCO Asia M&C Stella 2025 + 250+ events + Pico Group + HKEX heritage badges = strong; but hero lacks value prop (logo + nav only), navy-on-white conservative palette, multiple H2s fragment narrative, no client logo grid, news section overflows, contact form generic.

competitors.name: S-Lite Group
competitors.pricing_range_published: Bespoke; logistics-heavy components priced separately
competitors.primary_value_prop: Event management with deep in-house barricade, fabrication and print logistics.
strengths: Started 2002 as metal/water barricade specialist (per slitegroup.com) — physical-asset moat for outdoor events
In-house creative + craftsmen reduce subcontractor mark-up
Sports/marathon niche is high-margin counter-cycle to corporate D&D
weaknesses: Brand still reads logistics-first vs. creative-first
Sports niche ties revenue to event calendar volatility
No proven luxury / hospitality positioning

competitors.name: CEMS (Conference & Exhibition Management Services)
competitors.pricing_range_published: Show-organiser model + sponsor packages; figures private
competitors.primary_value_prop: 1980-founded SG MICE organiser of Safety & Security Asia, SIJS and Enviro Asia trade shows.
strengths: 200+ major conventions/exhibitions since 1980 (per cems.com.sg)
Owns brand IP for Safety & Security Asia, Singapore Intl Jewellery Show, Enviro Asia
Multi-country SEA + China footprint
weaknesses: Trade-show / exhibition orientation — does not pitch corporate D&D
Legacy brand voice; limited digital marketing surface
PCO model captures less of the experiential-event budget

competitors.name: Ace:Daytons Direct (Ad Planet Group)
competitors.pricing_range_published: Bespoke; integrated ATL+BTL services priced jointly
competitors.primary_value_prop: Multi-award marcom and PCO group offering integrated event + advertising solutions APAC-wide.
strengths: Singapore Tourism Awards 2017 winner — Best Business Event Service Provider (per acedaytons-direct.com)
Part of Ad Planet Group with 100+ creative awards in 4 years
Founded 1994; 31-year track record
weaknesses: Integrated marcom pitch competes for ad budget, not HR D&D budget
Premium positioning
Legacy mid-1990s brand voice
competitors.website_design_notes: Divi WordPress template (Open Sans 14px body — 2018-era stack); SG Tourism Awards 2017 + 100+ creative awards/Pioneer Awards 2019 mentioned; default Divi layout with award images, generic h1 30px/h2 26px, 14px body fails comfort on retina, slow/jQuery-shim pre-loader.

competitors.name: Jack Morton Worldwide (Singapore)
competitors.pricing_range_published: Enterprise / network-agency tier
competitors.primary_value_prop: Global brand experience network with SG hub for APAC enterprise activations.
strengths: IPG-backed network resources (per jackmorton.com); SG office serves APAC HQs
Award-winning experiential creative pedigree
Cross-border execution — wins regional roll-outs
weaknesses: Network-agency overhead inflates pricing 2-3x SG mid-market
Long approval chain for SME briefs
No SG temp-staffing or FMCG-promoter arm
competitors.website_design_notes: Singapore skyline hero + Adweek 2023 Experiential Agency of Year award + Disney/Netflix/TikTok/Google/Airbnb portfolio; navy/orange palette high contrast; named contacts (Phil Boyle/Cassandra Harris); but no client testimonials/team count, RFP CTA below fold.

competitors.name: GPJ Singapore (George P. Johnson)
competitors.pricing_range_published: Enterprise tier; large-scale production line items
competitors.primary_value_prop: Century-old experiential marketing network with SG APAC delivery hub.
strengths: 1,300+ staff across 30 offices, 6 continents (per gpj.com)
Worked Beijing 2008 + Shanghai Expo 2010 — global showcase pedigree
Serves 40+ Fortune 500 clients
weaknesses: Pricing structurally above SG SME / mid-market budget
Tech / auto vertical concentration — light on D&D-style HR events
US-centric P&L; SG office must justify regional uplift
competitors.website_design_notes: 40+ Fortune 500 logos (Netflix, Google, Meta, HSBC, Unilever, Porsche) carry the page; 'WORLD'S LEADING' H1 declarative but no supporting context; case study cards replace hero CTA; SwiftPic responsive imagery; no contact CTA above fold; minimal decorative variety reads sparse.

competitors.name: MCI Group (Singapore)
competitors.pricing_range_published: Enterprise tier; 350+ AI-driven activations in 2024
competitors.primary_value_prop: Global engagement and association events network with SG office for APAC delivery.
strengths: 60 offices in 31 countries (per mci-group.com)
350+ AI-driven campaigns delivered in 2024 — credible AI-event positioning
Healthcare / association vertical depth competes for NUH, NKF, SingHealth budgets
weaknesses: Geneva HQ pricing translates to enterprise SGD
Association-event muscle is overkill for SG SME D&D
No SG ground-staffing arm to match Elitez Group ecosystem
competitors.website_design_notes: Modern human-first H1 ('marketing communications collective'); 64 offices/34 countries + 5,100 projects + Adobe/Paris Olympics named cases; multiple soft CTAs ('Book a conversation'); contemporary sans-serif, responsive nav — global polish but not differentiated; SG-page localization weak.

competitors.name: Freeman Singapore (former FreemanXP)
competitors.pricing_range_published: Enterprise tier; trade-show construction priced per booth
competitors.primary_value_prop: Global brand experience network; legacy SG presence as 'The Event Company' pre-rebrand.
strengths: Operated SG market via 'The Event Company' before FreemanXP rebrand (per Marketing-Interactive 2017)
Largest US event-services parent — supply chain scale
Cross-border continuity for global clients exhibiting in SG
weaknesses: Brand confusion post-FreemanXP folding (2020+)
Trade-show centricity vs. corporate HR events
US-led P&L

competitors.name: BCD Meetings & Events (APAC)
competitors.pricing_range_published: Enterprise corporate-travel-anchored; meeting management retainer + per-event
competitors.primary_value_prop: Corporate travel parent BCD's meetings, incentives and events arm covering APAC.
strengths: Anchored to BCD Travel parent — wins captive corporate-travel buyer
Vertical depth in life sciences, medtech, financial services (per bcdme.com)
Founded 2006; global event-services category leader
weaknesses: Buyer is Procurement / Travel Mgr, not HR / Marketing — different door than Elitez
No SG-local family day or D&D specialism
Travel-procurement-driven margins

competitors.name: Reed Exhibitions / RX Singapore
competitors.pricing_range_published: Sponsor packages + booth fees; show-organiser revenue model
competitors.primary_value_prop: Global trade-show owner-operator with SG portfolio of B2B events.
strengths: RELX parent (FTSE-100) — capital and global brand IP
Owns recurring B2B trade-show franchises in SG
Strong sponsor sales engine
weaknesses: Trade-show economics — does not chase one-off corporate D&D
Buyer is exhibitor / sponsor, not HR / corporate-comms
London-led P&L

competitors.name: DIY: Hotel banquet teams (MBS / RWS / Shangri-La)
competitors.pricing_range_published: SGD 100-200/pax all-in for hotel ballroom + AV; F&B SGD 80-180/pax sit-down
competitors.primary_value_prop: Bypass agency layer; hotel banquet teams plan + execute D&D in-house.
strengths: Sands Grand Ballroom holds 6,000 banquet / 8,000 reception (per marinabaysands.com)
RWS column-free ballroom + 21 function rooms (per rwsentosa.com)
Bundled SGD 100-200/pax all-in undercuts agency-led mid-market pricing
weaknesses: No theming / creative concept beyond venue defaults
No team-building or family day capability
Buyer carries vendor coordination risk for entertainment + theming
competitors.website_design_notes: MBS Expo page bot-walled — scoring conservative. Hospitality megabrand template: cinematic hero photography, redundant carousel structure, capacity stats present (6000/8000), CTA scattered (mice@sands email + Enquire Now), heavy image weight likely.

competitors.name: DIY: In-house Corporate Communications team
competitors.pricing_range_published: Internal headcount cost only; venue + F&B booked direct
competitors.primary_value_prop: Run the event yourself — corp-comms or HR team coordinates vendors directly.
strengths: Zero agency margin — survey-cited 12-20% management fee saved (per funco.com.sg cost guides)
Institutional knowledge of internal stakeholders + brand guidelines
Government / stat boards have GeBIZ-procurement preference for direct vendor splits
weaknesses: DIY fails when 5+ vendors need coordinating (per getout.events DIY guide)
Internal team opportunity cost; no professional show-call backup
No event-specific insurance / risk-management infra
competitors.website_design_notes: gov.sg as DIY proxy. Government credibility maximal (institutional trust signal); hero rotates Budget 2026/NDR features; CTAs ('Find out more') blend with cards (low contrast); palette restrained gov.sg blue; functional sans-serif; mobile-responsive cards; not a buyer-conversion site.

competitors.name: DIY: Eventbrite + freelance MC + decorator bundle
competitors.pricing_range_published: Eventbrite free-paid tickets; freelance MC SGD 500-3,000; decorator SGD 600-1,500
competitors.primary_value_prop: Stitch together ticketing, freelance MC, decorator and venue — no agency layer.
strengths: Eventbrite ticketing free for free events, low % for paid
Local MC marketplace shows SGD 500-3,000 range (per luxoriaentertainment.com / funco.com.sg)
No agency contract; on-demand stitching
weaknesses: Vendor-coordination burden falls on internal team
No brand activation, family day or large-scale staffing capability
DIY breaks above ~100 PAX or with multi-day formats

competitors.name: Klook for Business
competitors.pricing_range_published: Per-seat experiences from SGD ~30-150 (Bounce, escape rooms, perfumery, pottery)
competitors.primary_value_prop: Pan-Asia experiences marketplace; book curated team-building activities by the seat.
strengths: Pan-APAC experiences supply (per klook.com/en-SG/blog) — broad activity menu
Self-serve digital booking eliminates RFP cycle
Per-seat economics let small teams trial before scaling
weaknesses: Activity-only — no D&D, gala, awards, family day production
Caps at sub-100 PAX before logistics fragmentation
Marketplace model — no single point of accountability

competitors.name: Mandai Wildlife Reserve (direct booking)
competitors.pricing_range_published: From SGD 1,200 minimum spend; venues 180 seat auditorium up to 1,300 standing
competitors.primary_value_prop: Wildlife-themed venues with in-house catering; book direct without an agency.
features: Tipi Tent at Night Safari (Evening in the Wild)
strengths: Unique IP — animals + nature + dining (per mandai.com)
From SGD 1,200 minimum spend — accessible price floor
In-house catering removes a vendor layer
weaknesses: Single-venue scope; no multi-day or off-site capability
Theming locked to wildlife; not suitable for premium brand activations
Limited to ~1,300 standing capacity — caps high-end Elitez briefs

competitors.name: Resorts World Sentosa Convention Centre (direct)
competitors.pricing_range_published: Hotel banquet bundles SGD 100-200/pax; column-free ballroom premium
competitors.primary_value_prop: Resort-style D&D venue with in-house event team — bypass external agency.
strengths: 50-5,000 PAX scale (per rwsentosa.com) covers Elitez's full ICP band
Unique attractions integration (Aquarium, Adventure Cove)
One-stop in-house event team removes agency mark-up
weaknesses: Single-location lock-in
Resort F&B premium adds 15-25% vs. hotel city venues
Limited theming creativity vs. agency-led brief
competitors.website_design_notes: Premium gold/black palette, capacity stats (6,500/1,361) + 4 award badges (Tourism Awards, GSTC Platinum); but 4 venue cards repeat 3x triggering carousel bloat, CTAs scattered (Enquire/email/Explore More), zero testimonials, mobile CTA hunt; clear 270deg projection USP.

competitors.name: Mileage Communications
competitors.pricing_range_published: Marcom retainer + project; 9 countries / 15 cities billing
competitors.primary_value_prop: Singapore's largest home-grown marcomm consultancy with bolt-on event execution.
strengths: Largest SG home-grown marcom consultancy (per mileage.com.sg) — incorporated 1992
Footprint in 9 countries / 15 cities lets it bid against IPG/Omnicom networks
Investor relations + IPO event experience commands premium billing
weaknesses: PR / comms-led — events are downstream of strategy retainer
Higher hourly rates than pure event boutiques
Limited family day / mass-event production muscle
